concentration.headline: Moderately concentrated — one leading city
concentration.body: Tehran leads Iran's urban system with 23.0% of tracked urban population across 499 cities — significant but not overwhelming. Secondary cities retain meaningful economic weight alongside the capital.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United Kingdom, Italy, Canada
concentration.contrast: Unlike: USA, Japan (more distributed) or Thailand, Argentina (more dominant)

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Tehran & Karaj
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Tehran and Karaj (41 km apart, combined population 16,121,470). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 41 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Iran sits between distributed and dominant: Tehran leads with 23.0% but doesn't overwhelm. Gravity clusters tightly around Tehran → Karaj (41 km avg) — a strong sub-regional core coexists with the national leader, creating a layered urban hierarchy.
